ProductAttributeItem.description:
Lightweight representation of a product's attribute value used in responses.

- attribute_id: the AttributeTable id
- attribute_name: translated main_name for display (optional, may be omitted in some endpoints)
- option_id: the AttributeOptionTable id when the value is an enum option
- option_value_key: the option key (e.g., "XS", "M") when applicable
ProductAttributeItem.title: ProductAttributeItem

ProductWithAttributes.description: Schema for a product along with its associated attributes.

get.description: Retrieve a category using its human-readable name (exact match, case-sensitive).

delete.description: Moves a category to the trash (restorable with `restore_category`). If products are still assigned to it the request fails with 409 and the product count; retry with `force=true` to also move all those products to the trash (restorable as one batch), or with `detach=true` to keep the products but clear their category. `force` and `detach` are mutually exclusive.

parameters.description: Also move all products in this category to the trash.
parameters.name: force

schema.description: Also move all products in this category to the trash.
schema.title: Force

parameters.description: Keep the products; clear their category reference instead.
parameters.name: detach

schema.title: Detach

schema.title: Response Get Available Attributes Shops  Shop Id  Categories  Category Id  Available Attributes Get

get.summary: Get available filter attributes for a category

get.description:
Fetch products in a category along with their attributes. Supports attribute-based filtering.

Attribute filters (mutually exclusive — only one can be used at a time):
* `option_id` array[UUID]: Filter by attribute option UUIDs.
* `attribute_id` UUID: Filter by attribute UUID.
* `option_value_key` array[str]: Filter by option value keys (e.g., 'S', 'RED').
* `attribute_name` str: Filter by attribute name.

post.description:
DEPRECATED: Create a new product attribute value for a product within a shop.

Notes:
- This endpoint is deprecated; prefer using the selected options endpoint when possible.

Validations:
- Product exists and belongs to the shop

delete.description: Delete a product attribute value if it belongs to the given shop.

get.description: Retrieve a single product attribute value by id scoped to the given shop.

post.description:
Create new product attribute value(s) for a specific product using product_id in the URL.

This deprecates the old POST / endpoint that required product_id in the body.

Notes:
- attribute_id is omitted; it will be inferred from option_id, as the option is already tied to an attribute_id.

- This endpoint first validates that all option_ids belong to attributes that belong to the shop.
- And that these options actually exist.
- If everything is passed, it will create the product attribute values.
- Duplicates are ignored, and the endpoint returns 201 Created for each successful creation. That means no 409 is raised when a duplicate is encountered; it just won't be created.
post.summary: Create product attribute values for product

put.description:
New version of selected options endpoint addressed by product_id in the path.

This endpoint now accepts option_ids that may belong to different attributes.
Requirements/validations:
  - product_id (path) must belong to the shop
  - option_ids must be a non-empty list
  - all option_ids must exist
  - every inferred attribute (from the options) must belong to the shop
  - for each inferred attribute, set selected options for (product_id, attribute) to exactly
    the provided option_ids that belong to that attribute
put.summary: Replace product attribute values for product

get.description: Returns all product-to-tag relationship records for a shop.

post.description: Create an association between a product and a tag. Both must exist within the shop.

get.description: Find the UUID of the association record between a specific product and tag. Returns 400 if the relation does not exist.
Retrieve a single product-to-tag association by its UUID.

delete.description: Delete the association between a product and a tag.

put.description: Update an existing product-tag association record.

get.description: Returns paginated products for a shop, each with its default price. Supports filtering (e.g. `featured:true`, `category_id:<uuid>`) and sorting via common query parameters.

parameters.description: Filter products by inventory state. `in_stock` returns products with stock > 0, `out_of_stock` returns products with stock = 0, `all` (default) returns everything.
parameters.name: stock_status

post.description: Add a new product to a shop category. The `order_number` is automatically set to the next available value within the category.

get.description:
Fetch a list of products along with their associated attributes.

You can filter the results using one of the following mutually exclusive attribute filters:

* `option_id` array[UUID]: Filter by one or multiple attribute option UUIDs.
* `attribute_id` UUID: Filter by attribute UUID.
* `option_value_key` array[str]: Filter by option value (e.g., 'Red', 'XL').
* `attribute_name` array[str]: Filter by one or multiple attribute names (e.g., 'Color', 'Size').

Only one attribute filter can be used at a time.

You can additionally narrow results by inventory state with `stock_status`:
`in_stock` (stock > 0), `out_of_stock` (stock = 0), or `all` (default).

parameters.description: Filter products by inventory state. `in_stock` returns products with stock > 0, `out_of_stock` returns products with stock = 0, `all` (default) returns everything.
parameters.name: stock_status

Response Get Multi With Attributes Shops  Shop Id  Products With Attributes Get

delete.description: Moves the product to the trash. The product disappears from all listings but can be restored with `restore_product` or via its revisions — this action is reversible. Only `force=true` permanently purges the product; that is irreversible and requires user (Cognito) credentials — API keys get 403.

parameters.description: Permanently purge instead of moving to trash. Irreversible.
parameters.name: force

schema.description: Permanently purge instead of moving to trash. Irreversible.
schema.title: Force

get.description: Retrieve full product details including all active prices, translations, and images. Public endpoint — no authentication required.

put.description: Update an existing product's details, including name, description, pricing, stock, and feature flags.

post.description: Brings a trashed (deleted) product back, including its tags and attribute values. If the product's category is also in the trash it is restored as well.

get.description: Returns the revision history of a product, newest first. Every change to the product (fields, translation, tags, attribute values, images) recorded one revision. Use `get_product_revision` to inspect a revision and `restore_product_revision` to roll back to it.
Returns one product revision including the full snapshot data (all fields, tags, attribute values and image references at that point in time).

post.description: Rolls the product back to the state captured in the given revision: fields, translation, category, tags, attribute values and image references. Soft-deleted categories/tags/attributes referenced by the snapshot are automatically restored from the trash; references that were permanently purged are matched by name where possible, otherwise reported in `unresolved`. The restore itself is recorded as a new revision, so it can be undone too. Read the returned report to see what could not be brought back.

parameters.description: Recreate the product from the snapshot even if it was permanently purged (user credentials required).
parameters.name: force

schema.description: Recreate the product from the snapshot even if it was permanently purged (user credentials required).
schema.title: Force

put.description: Move a product up (`move_up=true`) or down (`move_up=false`) in the display order within its category. Swaps `order_number` with the adjacent product.

get.description: Retrieve a single product together with its assigned attribute values (e.g. size, color). Public endpoint — no authentication required.

Shop-wide change feed: every recorded revision of every product, category, tag and attribute, newest first. Supports filtering by `entity_type` (product | category | tag | attribute), `entity_id`, `action` (create | update | delete | restore | baseline), `source` (rest | mcp) and `created_by`, plus `skip`/`limit` pagination (the total is returned in the Content-Range header). Use `get_revision` to inspect a revision's full snapshot.

parameters.description: Filter: product | category | tag | attribute.
parameters.name: entity_type

schema.description: Filter: product | category | tag | attribute.
schema.title: Entity Type

parameters.description: Filter on one specific entity.
parameters.name: entity_id

schema.description: Filter on one specific entity.
schema.title: Entity Id

parameters.description: Filter: create | update | delete | restore | baseline.
parameters.name: action

schema.description: Filter: create | update | delete | restore | baseline.
schema.title: Action

parameters.description: Filter: rest | mcp.
parameters.name: source

schema.description: Filter: rest | mcp.
schema.title: Source

parameters.description: Filter on actor, e.g. "api_key:<uuid>" or "cognito:<sub>".
parameters.name: created_by

schema.description: Filter on actor, e.g. "api_key:<uuid>" or "cognito:<sub>".
schema.title: Created By

get.summary: List all revisions in a shop

get.description: Returns one revision (of any entity type) by its id, including the full snapshot data. Use it to inspect entries from the `list_shop_revisions` feed.

post.description: Create a Stripe PaymentIntent for a one-time purchase. Uses the shop's own `stripe_secret_key`. The `price` is in euro cents. Returns a `clientSecret` to complete the payment on the frontend.

Create a Stripe Subscription for one or more products. Price lookup keys are resolved as `monthly-<product_id>` or `yearly-<product_id>`. Returns `clientSecret` and `subscriptionId` to confirm payment on the frontend.

delete.description: Immediately cancel a Stripe Subscription. Uses the shop's `stripe_secret_key`.

get.description: Returns all tags defined for a shop. Tags can be attached to products for flexible grouping and filtering.

post.description: Create a new tag for a shop. Tags are attached to products via the `products-to-tags` resource.

get.description: Retrieve a tag by its exact name within a shop.

delete.description: Moves a tag to the trash: it disappears from listings and from the products carrying it, but its product links are kept so restoring the tag brings everything back. This action is reversible. Only `force=true` permanently purges the tag (and its product links); that is irreversible and requires user (Cognito) credentials — API keys get 403.

parameters.description: Permanently purge instead of moving to trash. Irreversible.
parameters.name: force

schema.description: Permanently purge instead of moving to trash. Irreversible.
schema.title: Force

get.description: Retrieve a single tag by its UUID within a shop.

put.description: Update an existing tag's name or translations.

get.description: Returns all soft-deleted products and categories of the shop, so they can be inspected and restored.

schema.title: Response List Trash Shops  Shop Id  Trash Get
